pr.title: Match logic tables for character-specific items
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Matched the character-specific item logic table by moving the large it_803F3100 table out of raw assembly and into a new C translation unit, while preserving remaining adjacent data/string blobs in a companion assembly file. The PR also updated build object ordering and added a small comment marking the existing common item table.
what_changed: Removed the large raw .4byte table beginning at global label it_803F3100 from asm/melee/it/it_803F1418.s.
Added src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c containing extern ItemStateContainer declarations, callback prototypes, and struct ItemLogicTable it_803F3100[] initialized as C data.

Annotated the new C table with the section comment "Character-specific items" and included named entry comments for at least early identified items such as Mario fireball, Dr Mario pill, Kirby cutter beam, and Kirby hammer.
Kept NULL callback slots explicitly in the C initializer, preserving the exact sparse function-pointer layout visible in the deleted assembly.
Added asm/melee/it/it_803F3100.s for still-unmatched adjacent .data labels, including it_803F4CA8, it_803F4D20, and it_803F5428 string data.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c.o followed by asm/melee/it/it_803F3100.s.o after the existing it_803F1418 objects.
Changed src/melee/it/it_803F1418.c only to replace a blank line with the comment "// Common items" above struct ItemLogicTable it_803F14C4[43].
smart_moves: Split the original assembly region at a meaningful table boundary: the matched logic table became C, while nearby data that was not part of the matched table stayed in assembly to avoid overreaching.
Preserved object-file order in obj_files.mk so the new C object and residual assembly object could reproduce the original data layout.
Used the existing ItemLogicTable type instead of leaving the table as anonymous .4byte data, making the 15-slot item callback layout searchable and easier to audit.
Left placeholder NULL entries in place rather than compressing the table, which is critical for matching fixed-index dispatch tables.
Added item-name comments only where the table entries were identifiable from context, avoiding unsupported renaming of the many remaining anonymous entries.
decomp_lessons: Large data tables can often be matched by converting repeated .4byte callback groups into typed struct initializers while preserving every NULL and function pointer in original order.

When a source assembly blob contains multiple adjacent globals, it is safe to peel off the matched portion into C and keep the rest in a new assembly file, provided object ordering and alignment are preserved.

For item logic tables, retain the ItemStateContainer pointer as the first field and keep the callback slots aligned with the existing ItemLogicTable definition; do not infer extra semantic field names from sparse data alone.
A minimal build-system change can be part of the match: adding the new C object and residual assembly object in the right sequence is as important as the initializer contents.
Comments like "Common items" and "Character-specific items" are useful landmarks for later item-table cleanup without forcing premature symbol renames.
naming_conventions: Unidentified symbols remained address-based, e.g. it_803F3100, it_803F4CA8, it_803F4D20, it_803F5428, and many it_802xxxxx callbacks.
The typed table used the existing struct name ItemLogicTable and ItemStateContainer externs rather than introducing a new table type.
Known table entries were documented with human-readable comments such as Mario fireball, Dr Mario pill, Kirby cutter beam, and Kirby hammer, but the underlying symbols were not renamed.
The new source/assembly split followed the address-label filename convention: src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c and asm/melee/it/it_803F3100.s.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated a raw .4byte table into a C array initializer of struct ItemLogicTable.
Preserved .balign behavior by leaving residual assembly data in asm/melee/it/it_803F3100.s with explicit .section .data and alignment directives.
Maintained exact NULL placeholders in each logic-table row to match the original binary layout.
Declared external callback functions with bool or void return types as needed for C compilation while keeping their address-based names.
Used a companion assembly file for unmatched data/string labels after the table, rather than forcing all nearby data into C in one PR.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body was empty/null, so intent is inferred from the title and diff only.
searchable_terms: Match logic tables for character-specific items
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type the callbacks referenced by it_803F3100.
Search for follow-up work converting asm/melee/it/it_803F3100.s labels it_803F4CA8 and it_803F4D20 into C.
Compare common item table it_803F14C4 and character-specific table it_803F3100 to confirm ItemLogicTable field semantics.
Look for PRs that add headers/prototypes for item logic callbacks instead of local extern declarations.
